title: Conjecture 1.2
statement_text: Any $n$-vertex graph with fewer than $3n-6$ edges admits a bipartite cut, while some graphs with $3n-6$ edges do not. In particular, $\ell_{3}=3$.
context_text: The case $k=3$ was investigated by Bogdanov, Neustroeva, Sokolov, Volostnov, Russkin, and Voronov, who formulated this conjecture. The extremal examples are provided by $3$-trees. Partial progress on related statements (forest cuts and bipartite cuts) was obtained in the same work and subsequently by Botler et al. and Cheng, Tang and Zhan.
attributed_to: Bogdanov, Neustroeva, Sokolov, Volostnov, Russkin, and Voronov
paper_contribution: The paper does not directly prove or disprove Conjecture 1.2; it cites it as motivation for the general Conjecture 1.3, which the paper refutes for all sufficiently large $k$, showing that $\ell_k \sim \frac{k}{2}$ rather than $\ell_k = k$.
paper_title: On cuts of small chromatic number in sparse graphs

title: Conjecture 1.3
statement_text: For every integer $k$ and every graph $G$ on at least $k$ vertices, if $$|E(G)|<k|V(G)|-\tfrac{k(k+1)}{2},$$ then $G$ admits a cut $X$ with $\chi(G[X])<k$. In particular, $\ell_{k}=k$.
context_text: Motivated by the confirmed cases $\ell_1=1$, $\ell_2=2$ (Theorem 1.1), and the conjectured $\ell_3=3$ (Conjecture 1.2), the paper's authors propose this bold generalization asserting that $\ell_k=k$ for all $k$. The main purpose of the paper is then to show that this conjecture is far from correct.
notes: This conjecture is introduced by the paper's own authors and immediately refuted within the same paper: Theorem 1.4 shows $\ell_k \leq (1+\varepsilon)\frac{k}{2}$ for all sufficiently large $k$, so that asymptotically $\ell_k \sim \frac{k}{2}$ rather than $\ell_k = k$.
paper_title: On cuts of small chromatic number in sparse graphs
